2606.08831.title: Inference-Time Conformal Reasoning with Valid Factuality Control for Large Language Models
2606.08831.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly perform multi-step reasoning, where intermediate claims form implicit directed acyclic graphs whose node correctness is structurally conditioned on their ancestors. This makes factuality uncertainty structural, rather than a trivial accumulation of node-wise errors, and necessitates inference-time uncertainty quantification over the reasoning structure. While conformal prediction (CP) offers flexible user-specified factuality control, existing work remains post-hoc and cannot intervene during generation. To fill the gap between CP's flexibility and its post-hoc limitation, we propose an \emph{Inference-Time Conformal Reasoning (ITCR)} framework that integrates CP directly into reasoning graph generation. ITCR learns a structure-level factuality uncertainty function that aggregates claim-level factuality signals over reasoning graphs without complex modeling assumptions. We then design the non-conformity score based on graph-level factuality uncertainty and calibrate the conformal threshold to decide when to stop generation. We theoretically show such generation is nested, yielding valid coverage guarantees for factuality control. Experiments over multiple datasets and coverage objectives demonstrate empirically valid coverage. In downstream reasoning tasks, inference-time calibrated graphs yield more accurate generation than post-hoc pruned graphs.
2606.08831.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越能執行多步推理，其中中間主張形成隱式有向無環圖，其節點的正確性在結構上依賴於其祖先。這使得事實性不確定性成為結構性的，而不是節點錯誤的簡單累積，並且需要在推理結構上進行推理時的不確定性量化。雖然符合預測（CP）提供靈活的用戶指定事實性控制，但現有的工作仍然是事後的，無法在生成過程中進行干預。為了填補CP的靈活性與其事後限制之間的空白，我們提出了一個\emph{推理時符合推理（ITCR）}框架，將CP直接整合到推理圖生成中。ITCR學習一個結構級別的事實性不確定性函數，該函數在不需要複雜建模假設的情況下，聚合推理圖中的主張級別事實性信號。然後，我們基於圖級別的事實性不確定性設計不符合性分數，並校準符合閾值以決定何時停止生成。我們從理論上證明這樣的生成是嵌套的，為事實性控制提供有效的覆蓋保證。在多個數據集和覆蓋目標上的實驗實證了有效的覆蓋。在下游推理任務中，推理時校準的圖比事後修剪的圖產生更準確的生成結果。
2606.08831.author: Ting Wang et.al.
2606.08831.authors: Ting Wang, Yuanjie Shi, Yan Yan, Huan Zhang
